Package io.mdudel.zenoh.purejava.transport

Class StreamFramer

java.lang.Object
io.mdudel.zenoh.purejava.transport.StreamFramer
public final class StreamFramer extends Object
Stream-transport framing helpers for Zenoh 1.x over stream-oriented links (TCP, TLS, WSS).

Because TCP does not preserve message boundaries, Zenoh prepends every batch on the wire with a 16-bit little-endian length header indicating how many bytes of payload follow. This matches BatchSize = u16 in commons/zenoh-protocol/src/transport/mod.rs and the read loop in io/zenoh-transport/src/unicast/link.rs which reads the two length bytes then a body of exactly that many bytes.

 +---------------+---------------+~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~+
 |  len_lo (u8)  |  len_hi (u8)  |  payload (len bytes)|
 +---------------+---------------+~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~+
  \_______ 2-byte little-endian __/

MAX_FRAME_BYTES = 65 535 is a hard wire cap; payloads above that must be split into FRAGMENT messages (deferred to a later turn).

All methods are static and stateless. This class does not own the streams and does not close them; that is the transport's job.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final int
    LENGTH_PREFIX_BYTES
    Byte length of the length prefix itself (2).
    static final int
    MAX_FRAME_BYTES
    Maximum bytes of payload in a single framed batch, per the BatchSize=u16 wire cap.

    readFrame(InputStream in)
    Read one framed batch from the stream.

  • Field Details

    • MAX_FRAME_BYTES

      public static final int MAX_FRAME_BYTES
      Maximum bytes of payload in a single framed batch, per the BatchSize=u16 wire cap.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • LENGTH_PREFIX_BYTES

      public static final int LENGTH_PREFIX_BYTES
      Byte length of the length prefix itself (2).
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Method Details

    • writeFrame

      public static void writeFrame(OutputStream out, byte[] payload) throws IOException
      Write one framed batch (length-prefix + payload) to the stream. Does not flush; caller decides when to flush.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if payload.length exceeds MAX_FRAME_BYTES
      IOException - on write failure

    • readFrame

      public static byte[] readFrame(InputStream in) throws IOException
      Read one framed batch from the stream. Blocks until either the full frame is available or the stream ends / errors.
      Returns:
      the payload bytes (without the length prefix)
      Throws:
      EOFException - if the stream ends before a full frame is available. A clean end (EOF on the very first read of the length prefix) is signalled with the message "clean EOF at frame boundary"; a partial-frame EOF is signalled with a message including how many bytes were expected vs read.
      IOException - on read failure
